section.heading: Unfair or deceptive acts or practices
section.chapeau: The sale of a covered horse, or of any other horse in anticipation of its future participation in a covered race, shall be considered an unfair or deceptive act or practice in or affecting commerce under section 45(a) of this title if the seller—

paragraph.chapeau: knows or has reason to know the horse has been administered—

subparagraph.content: a bisphosphonate prior to the horse’s fourth birthday; or
any other substance or method the Authority determines has a long-term degrading effect on the soundness of the covered horse; and

paragraph.content: fails to disclose to the buyer the administration of the bisphosphonate or other substance or method described in paragraph (1)(B).
